metadata_update.scope: Workspace HTTP API callers only (/api/generate and /api/chat). CLI callers (`ollama run`) are out of scope and flagged separately.

files_modified.change: Added 'think':false at the top level of the /api/generate JSON request body (not inside options). Implemented via jq --argjson think false to ensure proper JSON type.

Added a shell case statement that sets THINK_FIELD=',"think":false' when the model name starts with 'gemma4'. Injected into the request body via shell parameter expansion. Preserves backward compatibility for non-gemma4 callers (deepseek, qwen, etc.).
files_modified.lines_affected: 144-157 (8 lines: 4-line comment + case statement + THINK_FIELD interpolation in curl -d)

test_results.notes: TKT-1016 root cause reproduced: 20 tokens generated, all consumed by hidden thinking, response field is empty string.
Bug fixed. Response is non-empty, eval_count is 2 (not 20). done_reason=stop (not length).
Bug fixed on 8B. Cold-load took 6.27s; answer generated in 0.18s after load.

test_results.request: extracted from patched crest-v1.3-judgment-benchmark.sh line 71 (jq -n ... --argjson think false)
test_results.notes: End-to-end smoke test of the EXACT curl+jq command from the patched script. Single-word prompt returns clean response.

test_results.response: [SUMMARY] Test input consisting of 50 lines of repetitive test text designed to trigger summarization.
test_results.notes: Full end-to-end pipeline works: input exceeds threshold → script injects think:false → call succeeds → response parsed → [SUMMARY] prefix added → log entry written. state/context-summary-log.json shows model=gemma4:31b, success=true, output=102c.

test_results.response: [SUMMARY] The content is 50 lines of repetitive test text with no key decisions, facts, action items, dates, names, or TODO/completed items.
test_results.notes: Non-gemma4 callers unchanged. case gemma4* doesn't match, so THINK_FIELD stays empty, request body identical to pre-patch behavior.

unpatched.reason: OUT_OF_SCOPE: uses /usr/local/bin/ollama run CLI (not HTTP API). The Ollama CLI does not honor the 'think:false' request body field — the equivalent CLI flag is 'ollama run --think=false' (or its variant). Task scope was explicitly HTTP API callers.
unpatched.recommended_action: Future work: add --think=false to the ollama run invocation in gemma4-benchmark.sh. Document in follow-up ticket; not a TKT-1017 concern.

unpatched.reason: OUT_OF_SCOPE: default model is qwen3:4b (not gemma4 family). Already uses /no_think prompt prefix to disable thinking at the prompt level.

OUT_OF_SCOPE: the OpenClaw Ollama provider plugin code is bundled vendor code, not in the workspace. The plugin does not currently inject think:false when calling /api/generate or /api/chat for gemma4. This means the OpenClaw agent runtime (when invoking gemma4 via the Ollama plugin) is also affected by the same TKT-1015/TKT-1016 bug.
unpatched.recommended_action: NEEDS_KEN: Either (a) raise a separate CHG to patch the OpenClaw Ollama provider vendor code to inject think:false for gemma4 family at the plugin level, OR (b) escalate upstream to OpenClaw maintainers. The workspace-script patches do not fix the gateway runtime path. This is a known gap that should be addressed before relying on gemma4 for production agent inference via the OpenClaw gateway.

additional_observations: Test 3 (PATCHED crest-v1.3 template via cloud route) revealed an unrelated downstream bug: when the model response contains a literal newline byte (0x0a) the JSON response from Ollama is not properly escaped, making it invalid for strict JSON parsers like jq and Python's json.load. This affects both local and cloud Ollama responses. The patch in TKT-1017 does NOT address this; it only fixes the think:false issue. The script's verdict parsing in crest-v1.3-judgment-benchmark.sh would still need to handle multi-line responses (e.g., by using jq -R or python's json with strict=False). This is a downstream bug separate from TKT-1016/TKT-1017 and should be tracked as a separate ticket if it impacts production.

Test results show the bug is fully fixed: response is non-empty for gemma4:31b (8B) and gemma4:latest (8B). gemma4:26b was NOT smoke-tested (cold model, would require unloading the 31B which is currently loaded) — the test was deferred to avoid disrupting the live loaded model. The patch applies generically to gemma4 family; the smoke test on 31B + 8B + cloud-routed 31B is sufficient evidence the fix is universal.
follow_up_recommendations: Ken: raise a separate ticket to patch the OpenClaw Ollama provider plugin vendor code to inject think:false at the provider level (so the agent runtime, not just the workspace scripts, benefits from the fix).
Ken: if Ollama PR #16850 (server-side default-think=false for gemma4) merges upstream, the workspace patches can be removed.
Yoda: update the model-routing skill / model-routing guidance to document the gemma4 + think:false requirement for any future caller.
Yoda: consider adding a regression test (e.g., in tests/regression/model-routing/) that asserts any workspace Ollama caller handling gemma4 includes think:false.
